name: Al Bowlly
abstract: Albert Allick Bowlly (7 January 1899 – 17 April 1941) was a British vocalist, crooner, and dance band guitarist who was Britain's most popular singer for most of the 1930s. He recorded upwards of 1,000 songs that were listened to by millions in Britain and other English speaking countries, seeing further success in the United States.

works_created.t: Hang Out the Stars in Indiana

about_quotes.speaker: Arthur Briggs
about_quotes.evidence: Al Bowlly, he came to Berlin with an orchestra, South African orchestra by the name of Adler.
